req.title: Hr-Admin views expense-claim
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a expense-claim through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for HR Admin Expense Claim View functionality.
Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view an expense-claim
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record
must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, date
from decimal import Decimal


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    """Return a fully authenticated HR-admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 1
    user.username = "hr_admin_test"
    user.email = "hr_admin@example.com"
    user.role = "hr-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_hr_admin_user():
    """Return an authenticated user without the hr-admin role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 2
    user.username = "regular_employee"
    user.email = "employee@example.com"
    user.role = "employee"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    user.is_active = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_expense_claim():
    """Return a complete, valid expense-claim record."""
    return {
        "id": 101,
        "claim_number": "EXP-2024-00101",
        "employee_id": 42,
        "employee_name": "Jane Doe",
        "department": "Engineering",
        "submission_date": date(2024, 6, 1),
        "expense_date": date(2024, 5, 31),
        "category": "Travel",
        "description": "Client visit to New York",
        "amount": Decimal("452.75"),
        "currency": "USD",
        "receipt_reference": "REC-2024-0501",
        "status": "Pending",
        "approver_id": 10,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def expense_claim_missing_mandatory_fields():
    """Return an expense-claim dict with mandatory fields absent/None."""
    return {
        "id": 102,
        "claim_number": None,       # mandatory – missing
        "employee_id": None,        # mandatory – missing
        "employee_name": "",        # mandatory – missing
        "department": "HR",
        "submission_date": None,    # mandatory – missing
        "expense_date": date(2024, 5, 30),
        "category": None,           # mandatory – missing
        "description": "",          # mandatory – missing
        "amount": None,             # mandatory – missing
        "currency": "USD",
        "receipt_reference": None,  # mandatory – missing
        "status": "Pending",
        "approver_id": 10,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    """Return a mock audit-log service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def expense_claim_service(valid_expense_claim):
    """Return a mock expense-claim service pre-loaded with one claim."""
    service = MagicMock()
    service.get_by_id.return_value = valid_expense_claim
    return service


@pytest.fixture
def expense_claim_form_view(
    expense_claim_service, audit_service
):
    """
    Instantiate the ExpenseClaimFormView under test, injecting mocked
    dependencies.  Import path is illustrative – adjust to your project.
    """
    from expense_claims.views import ExpenseClaimFormView  # adjust import

    view = ExpenseClaimFormView(
        claim_service=expense_claim_service,
        audit_service=audit_service,
    )
    return view


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – mandatory field names expected by the form
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

MANDATORY_FIELDS = [
    "claim_number",
    "employee_id",
    "employee_name",
    "submission_date",
    "category",
    "description",
    "amount",
    "receipt_reference",
]


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication & Authorisation
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthenticationAndAuthorisation:

    def test_hr_admin_can_access_view(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """An authenticated hr-admin receives an HTTP 200 when viewing a claim."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_unauthenticated_user_is_redirected(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, unauthenticated_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """An unauthenticated request must be redirected (HTTP 302/401)."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=unauthenticated_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        assert response.status_code in (302, 401)

    def test_non_hr_admin_receives_forbidden(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, non_hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """A user without the hr-admin role must receive HTTP 403."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=non_hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_inactive_hr_admin_is_rejected(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """An inactive hr-admin account must not be allowed access."""
        hr_admin_user.is_active = False
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        assert response.status_code in (302, 401, 403)

    def test_session_token_is_validated_on_each_request(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """Each view request must re-validate the session token (no caching bypass)."""
        hr_admin_user.session_token = "invalid-token"
        with patch(
            "expense_claims.views.validate_session_token", return_value=False
        ):
            response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
                user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
            )
        assert response.status_code in (302, 401)


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Expense Claim Retrieval
# ===========================================================================

class TestExpenseClaimRetrieval:

    def test_valid_claim_is_returned_in_response(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """The response context must contain the correct expense-claim data."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        assert response.context["expense_claim"]["id"] == valid_expense_claim["id"]
        assert (
            response.context["expense_claim"]["claim_number"]
            == valid_expense_claim["claim_number"]
        )

    def test_nonexistent_claim_returns_404(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, expense_claim_service
    ):
        """Requesting an unknown claim ID must return HTTP 404."""
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=99999
        )
        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_claim_service_called_with_correct_id(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_expense_claim,
        expense_claim_service,
    ):
        """The data layer must be queried with the exact claim ID from the URL."""
        expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.assert_called_once_with(
            valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )

    def test_all_claim_fields_present_in_form_context(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """Every field from the claim record must be surfaced in the form context."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        claim_in_context = response.context["expense_claim"]
        for field in valid_expense_claim:
            assert field in claim_in_context, (
                f"Field '{field}' not found in form context"
            )

    def test_claim_amount_is_correctly_formatted(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """Monetary amounts must be rendered with two decimal places."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        rendered_amount = response.context["expense_claim"]["amount"]
        assert rendered_amount == Decimal("452.75")


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Mandatory Field Validation
# ===========================================================================

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        expense_claim_service,
        valid_expense_claim,
        missing_field,
    ):
        """
        When a mandatory field is absent/empty the view must report a
        validation error and must NOT return HTTP 200.
        """
        claim = dict(valid_expense_claim)
        claim[missing_field] = None  # simulate missing value

        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = claim
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=claim["id"]
        )

        assert response.status_code != 200
        errors = response.context.get("form_errors", {})
        assert missing_field in errors, (
            f"Expected a validation error for mandatory field '{missing_field}'"
        )

    def test_all_mandatory_fields_present_yields_no_validation_errors(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """A fully populated claim must pass validation with zero errors."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        assert response.status_code == 200
        errors = response.context.get("form_errors", {})
        assert not errors, f"Unexpected validation errors: {errors}"

    def test_empty_string_treated_as_missing_for_text_fields(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        expense_claim_service,
        valid_expense_claim,
    ):
        """Empty strings in text mandatory fields must trigger validation errors."""
        claim = dict(valid_expense_claim)
        claim["description"] = "   "  # whitespace-only
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = claim

        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=claim["id"]
        )

        errors = response.context.get("form_errors", {})
        assert "description" in errors

    def test_zero_amount_is_invalid(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        expense_claim_service,
        valid_expense_claim,
    ):
        """An amount of zero must not pass mandatory-field / business validation."""
        claim = dict(valid_expense_claim)
        claim["amount"] = Decimal("0.00")
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = claim

        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=claim["id"]
        )

        errors = response.context.get("form_errors", {})
        assert "amount" in errors

    def test_negative_amount_is_invalid(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        expense_claim_service,
        valid_expense_claim,
    ):
        """A negative amount must fail validation."""
        claim = dict(valid_expense_claim)
        claim["amount"] = Decimal("-10.00")
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = claim

        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=claim["id"]
        )

        errors = response.context.get("form_errors", {})
        assert "amount" in errors

    def test_future_submission_date_is_invalid(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        expense_claim_service,
        valid_expense_claim,
    ):
        """A submission date in the future must be flagged as invalid."""
        claim = dict(valid_expense_claim)
        claim["submission_date"] = date(2099, 1, 1)
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = claim

        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=claim["id"]
        )

        errors = response.context.get("form_errors", {})
        assert "submission_date" in errors

    def test_multiple_missing_fields_all_reported(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        expense_claim_service,
        expense_claim_missing_mandatory_fields,
    ):
        """When several mandatory fields are absent, ALL errors must be reported."""
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = (
            expense_claim_missing_mandatory_fields
        )
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user,
            claim_id=expense_claim_missing_mandatory_fields["id"],
        )
        errors = response.context.get("form_errors", {})
        missing = [
            f
            for f in MANDATORY_FIELDS
            if not expense_claim_missing_mandatory_fields.get(f)
        ]
        for field in missing:
            assert field in errors, (
                f"Expected validation error for '{field}' but it was absent"
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Audit Record
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditRecord:

    def test_audit_record_written_on_successful_view(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_expense_claim,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """A successful view of a valid claim must create exactly one audit record."""
        expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        audit_service.log.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_action(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_expense_claim,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record action must identify the view event."""
        expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        call_kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args.kwargs
        assert call_kwargs.get("action") == "VIEW_EXPENSE_CLAIM"

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_user(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_expense_claim,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must capture the hr-admin's user ID."""
        expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        call_kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args.kwargs
        assert call_kwargs.get("user_id") == hr_admin_user.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_claim_id(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_expense_claim,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must reference the viewed claim ID."""
        expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        call_kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args.kwargs
        assert call_kwargs.get("resource_id") == valid_expense_claim["id"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_expense_claim,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must include a timestamp close to now."""
        before = datetime.utcnow()
        expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        after = datetime.utcnow()

        call_kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args.kwargs
        timestamp = call_kwargs.get("timestamp")
        assert timestamp is not None
        assert before <= timestamp <= after

    def test_no_audit_record_written_for_unauthenticated_user(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        unauthenticated_user,
        valid_expense_claim,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Failed (unauthenticated) access must NOT produce a success audit record."""
        expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=unauthenticated_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        # The audit service should either not be called or be called with a
        # failure/access-denied action – never with VIEW_EXPENSE_CLAIM.
        for c in audit_service.log.call_args_list:
            assert c.kwargs.get("action") != "VIEW_EXPENSE_CLAIM"

    def test_no_audit_record_written_on_validation_failure(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        expense_claim_service,
        expense_claim_missing_mandatory_fields,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """
        When mandatory field validation fails the view is not deemed successful
        and no success audit record must be created.
        """
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = (
            expense_claim_missing_mandatory_fields
        )
        expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user,
            claim_id=expense_claim_missing_mandatory_fields["id"],
        )
        for c in audit_service.log.call_args_list:
            assert c.kwargs.get("action") != "VIEW_EXPENSE_CLAIM"

    def test_no_audit_record_written_for_nonexistent_claim(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        expense_claim_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """A 404 response must not generate a success audit entry."""
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        expense_claim_form_view.get(user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=99999)
        for c in audit_service.log.call_args_list:
            assert c.kwargs.get("action") != "VIEW_EXPENSE_CLAIM"

    def test_audit_record_contains_resource_type(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_expense_claim,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must identify the resource type as 'expense_claim'."""
        expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        call_kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args.kwargs
        assert call_kwargs.get("resource_type") == "expense_claim"

    def test_audit_record_contains_user_role(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_expense_claim,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must capture the role of the acting user."""
        expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        call_kwargs = audit_service.log.call_args.kwargs
        assert call_kwargs.get("user_role") == "hr-admin"


# ===========================================================================
# 5. UI / Form Rendering
# ===========================================================================

class TestUIFormRendering:

    def test_correct_template_is_used(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """The view must render the designated expense-claim form template."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        assert "expense_claims/view_claim.html" in response.templates_used

    def test_form_is_read_only_for_view_action(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """All form fields must be rendered in read-only / disabled mode."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        form = response.context.get("form")
        assert form is not None
        for field_name, field in form.fields.items():
            assert field.widget.attrs.get("readonly") or field.widget.attrs.get(
                "disabled"
            ), f"Field '{field_name}' is not read-only"

    def test_page_title_contains_claim_number(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """The rendered page title must include the claim reference number."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        assert valid_expense_claim["claim_number"] in response.context.get(
            "page_title", ""
        )

    def test_back_navigation_link_present(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """The form must provide a back/return navigation control."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
        )
        assert response.context.get("back_url") is not None

    def test_validation_error_messages_displayed_in_context(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        expense_claim_service,
        valid_expense_claim,
    ):
        """When a validation error exists the human-readable message must be present."""
        claim = dict(valid_expense_claim)
        claim["amount"] = None
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = claim

        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=claim["id"]
        )
        errors = response.context.get("form_errors", {})
        assert "amount" in errors
        assert isinstance(errors["amount"], str) and len(errors["amount"]) > 0


# ===========================================================================
# 6. Edge Cases & Security
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCasesAndSecurity:

    def test_sql_injection_in_claim_id_is_rejected(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user
    ):
        """A non-integer claim ID (injection attempt) must return HTTP 400/404."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id="1; DROP TABLE expense_claims;--"
        )
        assert response.status_code in (400, 404)

    def test_very_large_claim_id_handled_gracefully(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, expense_claim_service
    ):
        """An extremely large claim ID that doesn't exist must return HTTP 404."""
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=2**63 - 1
        )
        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_concurrent_view_requests_each_produce_one_audit_record(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_expense_claim,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Each independent view request must produce exactly one audit record."""
        for _ in range(3):
            expense_claim_form_view.get(
                user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"]
            )
        assert audit_service.log.call_count == 3

    def test_csrf_protection_is_enforced(
        self, expense_claim_form_view, hr_admin_user, valid_expense_claim
    ):
        """POST requests without a valid CSRF token must be rejected."""
        response = expense_claim_form_view.post(
            user=hr_admin_user,
            claim_id=valid_expense_claim["id"],
            csrf_token=None,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (400, 403)

    def test_xss_payload_in_description_is_escaped(
        self,
        expense_claim_form_view,
        hr_admin_user,
        expense_claim_service,
        valid_expense_claim,
    ):
        """Script tags in claim data must be HTML-escaped before rendering."""
        claim = dict(valid_expense_claim)
        claim["description"] = "<script>alert('xss')</script>"
        expense_claim_service.get_by_id.return_value = claim

        response = expense_claim_form_view.get(
            user=hr_admin_user, claim_id=claim["id"]
        )
        rendered = response.content.decode("utf-8")
        assert "<script>" not in rendered
        assert "<script>" in rendered
```

---

## Key design decisions

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Fixtures** | Separate fixtures for hr-admin, non-admin, and unauthenticated users so each scenario is unambiguous. |
| **Parametrisation** | `@pytest.mark.parametrize` over `MANDATORY_FIELDS` ensures every mandatory field is individually covered without duplicating test bodies. |
| **Mocking** | `MagicMock` for both the claim-service and audit-service keeps tests fast and isolated from the database/logging subsystem. |
| **Audit assertions** | Tests assert on `call_args.kwargs` to verify the *content* of the audit record (action, user_id, resource_id, timestamp, resource_type, role), not just that the method was called. |
| **Negative audit tests** | Explicit tests confirm the success audit action `VIEW_EXPENSE_CLAIM` is **never** written on auth failure, validation failure, or 404. |
| **Security** | Edge-case tests cover SQL injection, XSS escaping, CSRF enforcement, and boundary values. |
| **Import path** | `from expense_claims.views import ExpenseClaimFormView` is illustrative – adjust to match your project structure. |

doc._raw_response:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for HR Admin Expense Claim View functionality.
Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view an expense-claim
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record
must be written on success.
"""

"""
Pytest tests for HR Admin Expense Claim View functionality.
Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view an expense-claim
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record
must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, date
from decimal import Decimal


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    """Return a fully authenticated HR-admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 1
    user.username = "hr_admin_test"
    user.email = "hr_admin@example.com"
    user.role = "hr-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_hr_admin_user():
    """Return an authenticated user without the hr-admin role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 2
    user.username = "regular_employee"
    user.email = "employee@example.com"
    user.role = "employee"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    return user

def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    user.is_active = False
    return user
